sources: docs/core/B2_KERNEL_HARDENING.md A1 (lines 96-105), proposed tests (−): 'A 0x0b payload truncated at every byte offset, and one with a single trailing byte ... no batch opens'

docs/spec/WIRE_FORMAT.md §4 Event layouts (normative) — 'a decoder MUST reject truncated payloads (at any byte offset) and trailing bytes'; §3 Event frame (normative) — 'A decoder MUST reject any event type byte not in the registry'

inputs.attackFlagText: Silent-skip means an almost-valid anchor is invisible rather than invalidating: an attacker can craft a payload that decodes under a buggy lenient decoder but not under the spec decoder — conformance must sweep near-miss encodings.

baseGoldenAnchor.note: 73 bytes, frame(5) ‖ prevRoot(32) ‖ newRoot(32) ‖ batchSize(u32). Pinned byte-identical against the BDK spike (canon Item 2). This vector REUSES the B1 wire golden + frame-sweep negatives at the @ont/consensus anchor-acceptance boundary rather than minting new bytes.

nearMissNegatives: truncation at every byte offset 1..72 (the B1 frame/event truncation sweep, re-asserted at the kernel boundary)
event type byte not in the registry (unassigned/reserved)
retired type 0x0d (was AvailabilityMarker; never reuse — rejected exactly like unassigned)
fixture.chainFacts: not consulted: decode-reject precedes any chain-fact or membership evaluation (a confirmed-tx context is assumed but never reached for a near-miss payload)
fixture.priorKernelState: any baseline; the post-input state MUST be byte-identical to it

inputs.scopeNote: Cited-now: WIRE_FORMAT §3 frame + §4/§4.4 RootAnchor exact 73-byte layout (normative). The kernel boundary re-asserts the B1 wire-decode law; no new rule. DELIBERATELY EXCLUDES (separate A1 attack flag, spec-blocked): the multiple-OP_RETURN-output skip-vs-reject / first-vs-all question — NOT asserted here.
inputs.authoringStatus: authored: predicate-input shape proposed (reuses the B1 wire golden + frame-sweep negatives); status stays proposed pending reviewer sign-off before lock.

expected.invariant: no batch opens; kernel state byte-identical to pre-input; decode-reject precedes chain-fact/membership evaluation
expected.rationale: This flag is about near-miss / almost-valid encodings being rejected by the spec decoder — exactly what A1's normative basis pins: decode EXACTLY per the wire spec, reject truncated/trailing/wrong-magic/wrong-version. A second implementer derives 'near-miss encodings are not anchor candidates' purely from normative WIRE_FORMAT §3/§4. Unlike the multi-output flag, this needs no missing rule — it is the byte-offset/near-miss sweep the rule already proposes as tests.

sources: docs/core/B2_KERNEL_HARDENING.md A4 (lines 132-140), proposed test (−): 'A served byte set whose leaf count contradicts the anchored batchSize does not satisfy the anchor's commitment'
docs/spec/ONT_DATA_AVAILABILITY_AGREEMENT.md §8b (candidate) — 'The anchor commits the batch (root + batchSize); every node verifies fetched bytes against that on-chain commitment'; §6b (candidate)

inputs.attackFlagText: Legacy code records batchSize for observability only and never cross-checks it against served leaves (indexer.ts:762) — a publisher can understate batchSize to shrink the Σ-gates the fee check requires while the root commits more leaves. The batchSize-consistency check must be stated explicitly at B2 hardening because the gate-fee rule's N must come from the commitment-checked batch, not the publisher's self-declared field.

acceptedAnchor.note: the anchored (newRoot, batchSize) pair fixes the authoritative leaf count N = 42

case_a.note: served set understates: count 41 != anchored batchSize 42

case_b.note: served set overstates: count 43 != anchored batchSize 42

fixture.chainFacts: anchor mined and confirmed (K-deep); fixed witnessed inputs
fixture.priorKernelState: baseline; the post-input state MUST be byte-identical to it (zero state effect)

inputs.scopeNote: Cited-now (candidate tier): DA §8b/§6b — verify fetched bytes against the on-chain (root + batchSize) commitment; fail-closed on a leaf-count contradiction (the 'fail-closed on absent/inconsistent evidence is vector-now even if witness format is B3' carve-out). DELIBERATELY EXCLUDES (spec-blocked, NOT asserted): (a) the served-bytes witness wire shape (typed hole, D-gaps / B3); (b) per-leaf membership-proof construction and what 'the bytes match the commitment' verifies (A4/A6 owner-binding, conflict C6 / B3). This vector asserts ONLY leaf-count vs anchored batchSize.
inputs.authoringStatus: authored: predicate-input shape proposed at candidate tier; the served-bytes witness FORMAT is a B3 hole, so the fixture pins only the count-vs-batchSize contradiction. reviewer sign-off complete; locked.

expected.invariant: the served set does not satisfy the anchor's commitment; the authoritative N for Σ-gates comes from the commitment-checked batch (42), never the served/self-declared count; zero state effect
expected.rationale: A4 is verdict 'cited' on candidate-tier DA agreement §8b/§6b: the anchor commits the batch via root + batchSize and a served byte set inconsistent with the commitment (including a leaf count contradicting batchSize) does not satisfy the anchor. The batchSize-consistency reject is directly derivable from this candidate authority (candidate text is acceptable where the verdict is candidate-stays/cited). No provisional decision and no invented rule: the served-bytes WITNESS FORMAT is B3, but the kernel's fail-closed posture on a batchSize-inconsistent leaf count is derivable now — this is the 'fail-closed on absent/inconsistent evidence is vector-now even if format is B3' carve-out.

sources: docs/core/B2_KERNEL_HARDENING.md A6 (lines 153-162) — 'The leaf-shape half stays cited'; proposed test (−): 'A leaf naming Alice (non-canonical bytes) is rejected, not normalized'
docs/spec/WIRE_FORMAT.md §2 Names (normative) — 'A decoder MUST reject a payload whose name bytes are non-canonical'

inputs.attackFlagText: Legacy normalizes leaf names instead of rejecting non-canonical input (indexer.ts:804-809) — direct conflict with the wire's reject-don't-normalize law; the witness format must mandate canonical name bytes.

leaf.note: a batched-claim leaf presenting a non-canonical name; the kernel must REJECT, not normalize to 'alice'

fixture.chainFacts: anchor accepted; leaf presented for merge against the anchored root
fixture.priorKernelState: baseline; the post-input state MUST be byte-identical to it (zero state effect)

inputs.scopeNote: Cited-now (normative): WIRE_FORMAT §2 — 'a name inside any encoded payload appears only in canonical form; a decoder MUST reject a payload whose name bytes are non-canonical (e.g. A-Z)'; A6's leaf-SHAPE half is cited. DELIBERATELY EXCLUDES (A6 candidate-stays / spec-blocked, conflict C6, NOT asserted): the owner-binding construction (H(ownerPubkey) vs raw ownerPubkey). This vector asserts ONLY reject-don't-normalize of the leaf name bytes.
inputs.authoringStatus: authored: predicate-input shape proposed; byte-pinned non-canonical name. reviewer sign-off complete; locked.

expected.invariant: non-canonical name bytes are REJECTED, never normalized to 'alice'; the kernel/leaf boundary mirrors WIRE_FORMAT §2 reject-don't-normalize; zero state effect
expected.rationale: A6's leaf-SHAPE half stays cited and this flag is squarely on it: reject-don't-normalize for non-canonical name bytes. The authority is normative WIRE_FORMAT §2 ('a name inside any encoded payload appears only in canonical form; a decoder MUST reject a payload whose name bytes are non-canonical'). A second implementer derives 'a leaf naming Alice (non-canonical bytes) is rejected, not normalized' directly from this normative law. No provisional decision, no missing rule — the kernel pins the wire's reject-don't-normalize law at the leaf boundary.

sources: docs/core/B2_KERNEL_HARDENING.md A10 (lines 199-208), proposed test (−): 'The predicate's type signature admits no wall-time, URL, or callback parameter ... the legacy batchDataProvider seam MUST NOT reappear inside the kernel'
docs/core/SOFTWARE_CANON.md The boundary rule (ratified) — 'pure predicate over witnessed inputs'; L2 ownership kernel 'No DB, no network, no clock, no UI'

inputs.attackFlagText: DA §6c's challenge phrasing tempts an implementation to poll mirrors at verdict time; the served-evidence must arrive as a B3-witnessed input or purity is silently broken.
inputs.predicateSignature: anchorAcceptance(eventBytes, witnessedChainFacts{minedHeight,txid,positionInBlock,confirmationDepth,txFeeSats}, priorKernelState, witnessedServedEvidence) -> {verdict, reason} — servedEvidence arrives as a WITNESSED INPUT, never fetched live

fixture.kind: structural/architectural negative — not a byte-input/verdict fixture
fixture.assertion: the anchor-acceptance/DA predicate's type signature admits NO wall-time, URL, or callback parameter; @ont/consensus production modules import no host I/O / network / process / timer / clock channel and read no host-time/network/storage global; the legacy batchDataProvider live-fetch seam cannot reappear inside the kernel
fixture.dischargedBy: packages/consensus/src/b2-boundary.test.ts (executable zero-I/O purity gate, committed @ 8459041) + the A10 predicate-signature constraint

inputs.scopeNote: Cited-now (ratified): SOFTWARE_CANON boundary rule + L2 'No DB, no network, no clock, no UI'. Discharged by the committed purity gate. DELIBERATELY EXCLUDES (deferred): the B3 served-evidence witness FORMAT — this vector asserts ONLY the purity posture / signature constraint, not the evidence shape.
inputs.authoringStatus: authored: structural vector — the executable discharge is the committed b2-boundary.test.ts; status stays proposed pending reviewer sign-off on the signature-constraint framing.

expected.invariant: an availability decision cannot be 'checked live' at verdict time; served-evidence must be a B3-witnessed input. The 'reject' is structural: the impure construction (mirror-poll / URL / callback / wall-clock in the predicate) is inexpressible at the boundary
expected.rationale: A10 is verdict 'cited' on the ratified SOFTWARE_CANON boundary rule (the anchor-acceptance verdict is a pure predicate over witnessed inputs; 'No DB, no network, no clock, no UI'). This flag — that §6c must NOT be implemented as a live mirror-poll, the served-evidence must arrive as a witnessed input — is the fail-closed-purity posture, derivable now from ratified authority. The B3 evidence FORMAT is deferred, but the kernel's purity posture (no network fetch in the predicate, type signature admits no URL/callback) is exactly the vector-now carve-out: purity on absent-live-fetch is derivable even though the witness format is B3.
